reasoning: The FRED DAAA corporate bond yield has spiked sharply from 5.25% on April 4th to 5.67% on April 11th, likely due to a recent hot CPI report causing Treasury yields to rise. The value on April 13th will be based on this very high 5.67% level. Because it has spiked almost 50 basis points in just a week, this level represents a local maximum, increasing the likelihood of mean reversion in the near term. The statistical trend analysis from the FRED tool estimates a roughly 31% chance of the yield exceeding 5.67 by April 20. Following the principle of increasing uncertainty over time, I expect the probability of the yield remaining above 5.67% to gradually drift back towards 0.5 for the later resolution dates. Therefore, my progression of probabilities starts low for the near-term date, and increases towards uncertainty for the furthest date.
